title: Making Agricultural Products Locally Essential (MAPLE) Act
short_title: Making Agricultural Products Locally Essential (MAPLE) Act
stage_label: Referred to the Subcommittee on Nutrition and Foreign Agriculture.
plain_summary: The MAPLE Act would add maple syrup to a government program that helps low-income seniors buy healthy food. Right now, seniors in this program can use special coupons to buy fresh fruits, vegetables, herbs, and honey at farmers markets and farm stands. This bill would let them also use those same coupons to buy maple syrup from local farms. The program helps seniors eat better while supporting local farmers in their communities.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Nutrition and Foreign Agriculture.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Making Agricultural Products Locally Essential Act or the MAPLE Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Expansion of the Seniors Farmers' Market Nutrition Program
sections.text: This section changes an existing law about the Seniors Farmers' Market Nutrition Program. The program currently helps seniors buy fresh fruits, vegetables, and herbs at farmers' markets. This change adds maple syrup to the list of foods that seniors can buy with program benefits. The change is made by updating the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of two thousand two.
